item.title: Peugeot maker Stellantis to phase out car assembly at historic Poissy plant
item.description: Move comes before chief executive Antonio Filosa unveils plan to turn around sprawling group

item.title: Who’s managing all the sovereign wealth?
item.description: We tried a hard thing and came away with more questions than answers

item.title: EasyJet warns of bigger than expected loss from jet fuel costs
item.description: Fuel prices have doubled in month since start of Iran war

item.title: Kering aims to double margins in turnaround push
item.description: Sales at luxury group’s biggest brand Gucci have fallen sharply in an industry-wide downturn

item.title: Taiwan overtakes UK in stock market value on AI chip boom
item.description: Crossover comes as chipmaker TSMC reports record first-quarter profit

item.title: Tesco warns Middle East war is clouding outlook
item.description: UK’s largest supermarket chain posts flat full-year profits

item.title: China’s surging chip tool imports from south-east Asia
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Does AI want to be free?
item.description: Plus, global imbalances and global crises

item.title: China’s battery champion pushes deeper into mining
item.description: CATL puts $4.4bn into new subsidiary to strengthen supply chain self-sufficiency

item.title: FirstFT: Nato in ‘turf war’ with EU over defence spending
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Repsol’s Venezuelan assets and Gulf states’ wartime borrowing

item.title: China shock 2.0: the countries feeling the ‘Chinese squeeze’
item.description: The sheer scale of the manufacturing superpower threatens to shut out south-east Asian rivals before they can rise

item.title: The financier atop London’s property market
item.description: Suneil Setiya of Quadrature Capital is revealed as the buyer of Nick Candy’s £275mn London mansion

item.title: Spain’s Repsol wins back control of Venezuelan oil operations
item.description: Deal with South American nation’s government includes guaranteed payment system and plan to triple production

item.title: Nissan held talks with China’s Chery on building cars in Sunderland
item.description: Legacy carmakers are battling to save jobs at European plants by partnering with newer rivals

item.title: UK lenders begin cutting mortgage rates after month of turmoil
item.description: Santander, TSB and Coventry are among the lenders to reduce costs of their fixed-rate deals this week

item.title: Hermès bumps up against luxury’s scarcity paradox
item.description: There is a tricky balance to strike between exclusivity and scale

item.title: DR Congo tightens control over critical minerals with strategic reserves
item.description: New stockpiles of cobalt, coltan and germanium are latest step by African countries for greater leverage over metals

item.title: Nato in ‘turf war’ with EU over defence spending
item.description: At the heart of the dispute is buying US weapons with EU money

item.title: Gulf states turn to private deals in $10bn wartime borrowing spree
item.description: Abu Dhabi, Qatar and Kuwait avoid public markets for fundraising as Iran conflict delivers economic hit

item.title: How the Strait of Hormuz will change Iran’s regime
item.description: A system fixated on building up military deterrents has discovered its most important point of leverage over the world

item.title: EU climate chief warns there is ‘no workaround’ for high energy prices
item.description: Crisis shows Europe’s need to reduce dependence on imported fossil fuels, says Wopke Hoekstra

item.title: British investor builds $500mn stake in TikTok owner
item.description: Fred Blackford bets on valuation gap between China’s ByteDance and its Silicon Valley rivals

item.title: The NHS should keep Palantir for patients’ sake
item.description: The US technology company is opposed by some employees but its software is getting results

item.title: ‘$20bn in 20 minutes’: the man turning Trump into a global deal machine
item.description: Paolo Zampolli provides access to the US president and his officials — at a price

item.title: a16z’s Martin Casado: It’s not that hard to build AI models
item.description: The technologist and investor argues recent progress in AI is an industrial revolution-scale event but warns the ability of the bigger players to raise ‘cheap money’ is time-limited

item.title: The tax-focused hedge fund craze taking over Wall Street
item.description: New breed of products from firms such as AQR and Quantinno has soared in popularity, raising regulatory concerns

item.title: Pentagon held talks with Ford and GM about supporting weapons production
item.description: Defence secretary Pete Hegseth has pushed to overhaul US military procurement

item.title: Thomson Reuters faces growing pressure over US immigration work
item.description: Shareholders challenge work for ICE after ex-employee sues legal database provider

item.title: China to drive a weight-loss drug price war
item.description: The active ingredient semaglutide is starting to lose patent protection and China can produce GLP-1 related treatments at scale

item.title: Goldman president warns private credit funds are not marketed properly
item.description: John Waldron says vehicles lack ‘clarity that this is really not a liquid product’
